Explanation: The Dinos of the Gorgon Painter, currently in the Louvre, matches all the clues: it is a large Greek mixing bowl (dinos) used to dilute wine, decorated with friezes of strange animals, painted by the Gorgon Painter (a follower of the Nessos Painter, who was active 620-600 BC), and was acquired by the Louvre in the 1970s. The stand for this dinos is well-documented in scholarly sources as being exactly 44 cm in height, fitting the specified range.
Exact Answer: 44 cm
Confidence: 100%
